NKJV.1: Moreover, brethren, I declare to you the gospel which I preached to you, which also you received and in which you stand,
NKJV.2: by which also you are saved, if you hold fast that word which I preached to you—unless you believed in vain.
NKJV.3: For I delivered to you first of all that which I also received: that Christ died for our sins according to the Scriptures,
NKJV.4: and that He was buried, and that He rose again the third day according to the Scriptures,
NKJV.5: and that He was seen by Cephas, then by the twelve.
NKJV.6: After that He was seen by over five hundred brethren at once, of whom the greater part remain to the present, but some have fallen asleep.
NKJV.7: After that He was seen by James, then by all the apostles.
NKJV.8: Then last of all He was seen by me also, as by one born out of due time.
NKJV.9: For I am the least of the apostles, who am not worthy to be called an apostle, because I persecuted the church of God.
NKJV.10: But by the grace of God I am what I am, and His grace toward me was not in vain; but I labored more abundantly than they all, yet not I, but the grace of God which was with me.
NKJV.11: Therefore, whether it was I or they, so we preach and so you believed.
NKJV.12: Now if Christ is preached that He has been raised from the dead, how do some among you say that there is no resurrection of the dead?
NKJV.13: But if there is no resurrection of the dead, then Christ is not risen.
NKJV.14: And if Christ is not risen, then our preaching is empty and your faith is also empty.
NKJV.15: Yes, and we are found false witnesses of God, because we have testified of God that He raised up Christ, whom He did not raise up—if in fact the dead do not rise.
NKJV.16: For if the dead do not rise, then Christ is not risen.
NKJV.17: And if Christ is not risen, your faith is futile; you are still in your sins!
NKJV.18: Then also those who have fallen asleep in Christ have perished.
NKJV.19: If in this life only we have hope in Christ, we are of all men the most pitiable.
NKJV.20: But now Christ is risen from the dead, and has become the firstfruits of those who have fallen asleep.
NKJV.21: For since by man came death, by Man also came the resurrection of the dead.
NKJV.22: For as in Adam all die, even so in Christ all shall be made alive.
NKJV.23: But each one in his own order: Christ the firstfruits, afterward those who are Christ’s at His coming.
NKJV.24: Then comes the end, when He delivers the kingdom to God the Father, when He puts an end to all rule and all authority and power.
NKJV.25: For He must reign till He has put all enemies under His feet.
NKJV.26: The last enemy that will be destroyed is death.
NKJV.27: For “He has put all things under His feet.” But when He says “all things are put under Him,” it is evident that He who put all things under Him is excepted.
NKJV.28: Now when all things are made subject to Him, then the Son Himself will also be subject to Him who put all things under Him, that God may be all in all.
NKJV.29: Otherwise, what will they do who are baptized for the dead, if the dead do not rise at all? Why then are they baptized for the dead?
NKJV.30: And why do we stand in jeopardy every hour?
NKJV.31: I affirm, by the boasting in you which I have in Christ Jesus our Lord, I die daily.
NKJV.32: If, in the manner of men, I have fought with beasts at Ephesus, what advantage is it to me? If the dead do not rise, “Let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we die!”
NKJV.33: Do not be deceived: “Evil company corrupts good habits.”
NKJV.34: Awake to righteousness, and do not sin; for some do not have the knowledge of God. I speak this to your shame.
NKJV.35: But someone will say, “How are the dead raised up? And with what body do they come?”
NKJV.36: Foolish one, what you sow is not made alive unless it dies.
NKJV.37: And what you sow, you do not sow that body that shall be, but mere grain—perhaps wheat or some other grain.
NKJV.38: But God gives it a body as He pleases, and to each seed its own body.
NKJV.39: All flesh is not the same flesh, but there is one kind of flesh of men, another flesh of animals, another of fish, and another of birds.
NKJV.40: There are also celestial bodies and terrestrial bodies; but the glory of the celestial is one, and the glory of the terrestrial is another.
NKJV.41: There is one glory of the sun, another glory of the moon, and another glory of the stars; for one star differs from another star in glory.
NKJV.42: So also is the resurrection of the dead. The body is sown in corruption, it is raised in incorruption.
NKJV.43: It is sown in dishonor, it is raised in glory. It is sown in weakness, it is raised in power.
NKJV.44: It is sown a natural body, it is raised a spiritual body. There is a natural body, and there is a spiritual body.
NKJV.45: And so it is written, “The first man Adam became a living being.” The last Adam became a life-giving spirit.
NKJV.46: However, the spiritual is not first, but the natural, and afterward the spiritual.
NKJV.47: The first man was of the earth, made of dust; the second Man is the Lord from heaven.
NKJV.48: As was the man of dust, so also are those who are made of dust; and as is the heavenly Man, so also are those who are heavenly.
NKJV.49: And as we have borne the image of the man of dust, we shall also bear the image of the heavenly Man.
NKJV.50: Now this I say, brethren, that flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of God; nor does corruption inherit incorruption.
NKJV.51: Behold, I tell you a mystery: We shall not all sleep, but we shall all be changed—
NKJV.52: in a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trumpet. For the trumpet will sound, and the dead will be raised incorruptible, and we shall be changed.
NKJV.53: For this corruptible must put on incorruption, and this mortal must put on immortality.
NKJV.54: So when this corruptible has put on incorruption, and this mortal has put on immortality, then shall be brought to pass the saying that is written: “Death is swallowed up in victory.”
NKJV.55: “O Death, where is your sting? O Hades, where is your victory?”
NKJV.56: The sting of death is sin, and the strength of sin is the law.
NKJV.57: But thanks be to God, who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.
NKJV.58: Therefore, my beloved brethren, be steadfast, immovable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, knowing that your labor is not in vain in the Lord.

ESV.1: Now I would remind you, brothers,  of the gospel I preached to you, which you received, in which you stand,
ESV.2: and by which you are being saved, if you hold fast to the word I preached to you — unless you believed in vain.
ESV.3: For I delivered to you as of first importance what I also received: that Christ died for our sins in accordance with the Scriptures,
ESV.4: that he was buried, that he was raised on the third day in accordance with the Scriptures,
ESV.5: and that he appeared to Cephas, then to the twelve.
ESV.6: Then he appeared to more than five hundred brothers at one time, most of whom are still alive, though some have fallen asleep.
ESV.7: Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles.
ESV.8: Last of all, as to one untimely born, he appeared also to me.
ESV.9: For I am the least of the apostles, unworthy to be called an apostle, because I persecuted the church of God.
ESV.10: But by the grace of God I am what I am, and his grace toward me was not in vain. On the contrary, I worked harder than any of them, though it was not I, but the grace of God that is with me.
ESV.11: Whether then it was I or they, so we preach and so you believed.
ESV.12: Now if Christ is proclaimed as raised from the dead, how can some of you say that there is no resurrection of the dead?
ESV.13: But if there is no resurrection of the dead, then not even Christ has been raised.
ESV.14: And if Christ has not been raised, then our preaching is in vain and your faith is in vain.
ESV.15: We are even found to be misrepresenting God, because we testified about God that he raised Christ, whom he did not raise if it is true that the dead are not raised.
ESV.16: For if the dead are not raised, not even Christ has been raised.
ESV.17: And if Christ has not been raised, your faith is futile and you are still in your sins.
ESV.18: Then those also who have fallen asleep in Christ have perished.
ESV.19: If in Christ we have hope  in this life only, we are of all people most to be pitied.
ESV.20: But in fact Christ has been raised from the dead, the firstfruits of those who have fallen asleep.
ESV.21: For as by a man came death, by a man has come also the resurrection of the dead.
ESV.22: For as in Adam all die, so also in Christ shall all be made alive.
ESV.23: But each in his own order: Christ the firstfruits, then at his coming those who belong to Christ.
ESV.24: Then comes the end, when he delivers the kingdom to God the Father after destroying every rule and every authority and power.
ESV.25: For he must reign until he has put all his enemies under his feet.
ESV.26: The last enemy to be destroyed is death.
ESV.27: For “God  has put all things in subjection under his feet.” But when it says, “all things are put in subjection,” it is plain that he is excepted who put all things in subjection under him.
ESV.28: When all things are subjected to him, then the Son himself will also be subjected to him who put all things in subjection under him, that God may be all in all.
ESV.29: Otherwise, what do people mean by being baptized on behalf of the dead? If the dead are not raised at all, why are people baptized on their behalf?
ESV.30: Why are we in danger every hour?
ESV.31: I protest, brothers, by my pride in you, which I have in Christ Jesus our Lord, I die every day!
ESV.32: What do I gain if, humanly speaking, I fought with beasts at Ephesus? If the dead are not raised, “Let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we die.”
ESV.33: Do not be deceived: “Bad company ruins good morals.”
ESV.34: Wake up from your drunken stupor, as is right, and do not go on sinning. For some have no knowledge of God. I say this to your shame.
ESV.35: But someone will ask, “How are the dead raised? With what kind of body do they come?”
ESV.36: You foolish person! What you sow does not come to life unless it dies.
ESV.37: And what you sow is not the body that is to be, but a bare kernel, perhaps of wheat or of some other grain.
ESV.38: But God gives it a body as he has chosen, and to each kind of seed its own body.
ESV.39: For not all flesh is the same, but there is one kind for humans, another for animals, another for birds, and another for fish.
ESV.40: There are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies, but the glory of the heavenly is of one kind, and the glory of the earthly is of another.
ESV.41: There is one glory of the sun, and another glory of the moon, and another glory of the stars; for star differs from star in glory.
ESV.42: So is it with the resurrection of the dead. What is sown is perishable; what is raised is imperishable.
ESV.43: It is sown in dishonor; it is raised in glory. It is sown in weakness; it is raised in power.
ESV.44: It is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body. If there is a natural body, there is also a spiritual body.
ESV.45: Thus it is written, “The first man Adam became a living being”;  the last Adam became a life-giving spirit.
ESV.46: But it is not the spiritual that is first but the natural, and then the spiritual.
ESV.47: The first man was from the earth, a man of dust; the second man is from heaven.
ESV.48: As was the man of dust, so also are those who are of the dust, and as is the man of heaven, so also are those who are of heaven.
ESV.49: Just as we have borne the image of the man of dust, we shall  also bear the image of the man of heaven.
ESV.50: I tell you this, brothers: flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of God, nor does the perishable inherit the imperishable.
ESV.51: Behold! I tell you a mystery. We shall not all sleep, but we shall all be changed,
ESV.52: in a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trumpet. For the trumpet will sound, and the dead will be raised imperishable, and we shall be changed.
ESV.53: For this perishable body must put on the imperishable, and this mortal body must put on immortality.
ESV.54: When the perishable puts on the imperishable, and the mortal puts on immortality, then shall come to pass the saying that is written: “Death is swallowed up in victory.”
ESV.55: “O death, where is your victory? O death, where is your sting?”
ESV.56: The sting of death is sin, and the power of sin is the law.
ESV.57: But thanks be to God, who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.
ESV.58: Therefore, my beloved brothers, be steadfast, immovable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, knowing that in the Lord your labor is not in vain.

NASB.1: Now I make known to you, brethren, the gospel which I preached to you, which also you received, in which also you stand,
NASB.2: by which also you are saved, if you hold fast the word which I preached to you, unless you believed in vain.
NASB.3: For I delivered to you as of first importance what I also received, that Christ died for our sins according to the Scriptures,
NASB.4: and that He was buried, and that He was raised on the third day according to the Scriptures,
NASB.5: and that He appeared to Cephas, then to the twelve.
NASB.6: After that He appeared to more than five hundred brethren at one time, most of whom remain until now, but some have fallen asleep;
NASB.7: then He appeared to James, then to all the apostles;
NASB.8: and last of all, as to one untimely born, He appeared to me also.
NASB.9: For I am the least of the apostles, and not fit to be called an apostle, because I persecuted the church of God.
NASB.10: But by the grace of God I am what I am, and His grace toward me did not prove vain; but I labored even more than all of them, yet not I, but the grace of God with me.
NASB.11: Whether then [it was] I or they, so we preach and so you believed.
NASB.12: Now if Christ is preached, that He has been raised from the dead, how do some among you say that there is no resurrection of the dead?
NASB.13: But if there is no resurrection of the dead, not even Christ has been raised;
NASB.14: and if Christ has not been raised, then our preaching is vain, your faith also is vain.
NASB.15: Moreover we are even found [to be] false witnesses of God, because we testified against God that He raised Christ, whom He did not raise, if in fact the dead are not raised.
NASB.16: For if the dead are not raised, not even Christ has been raised;
NASB.17: and if Christ has not been raised, your faith is worthless; you are still in your sins.
NASB.18: Then those also who have fallen asleep in Christ have perished.
NASB.19: If we have hoped in Christ in this life only, we are of all men most to be pitied.
NASB.20: But now Christ has been raised from the dead, the first fruits of those who are asleep.
NASB.21: For since by a man [came] death, by a man also [came] the resurrection of the dead.
NASB.22: For as in Adam all die, so also in Christ all will be made alive.
NASB.23: But each in his own order: Christ the first fruits, after that those who are Christ's at His coming,
NASB.24: then [comes] the end, when He hands over the kingdom to the God and Father, when He has abolished all rule and all authority and power.
NASB.25: For He must reign until He has put all His enemies under His feet.
NASB.26: The last enemy that will be abolished is death.
NASB.27: For HE HAS PUT ALL THINGS IN SUBJECTION UNDER HIS FEET. But when He says, 'All things are put in subjection,' it is evident that He is excepted who put all things in subjection to Him.
NASB.28: When all things are subjected to Him, then the Son Himself also will be subjected to the One who subjected all things to Him, so that God may be all in all.
NASB.29: Otherwise, what will those do who are baptized for the dead? If the dead are not raised at all, why then are they baptized for them?
NASB.30: Why are we also in danger every hour?
NASB.31: I affirm, brethren, by the boasting in you which I have in Christ Jesus our Lord, I die daily.
NASB.32: If from human motives I fought with wild beasts at Ephesus, what does it profit me? If the dead are not raised, LET US EAT AND DRINK, FOR TOMORROW WE DIE.
NASB.33: Do not be deceived: 'Bad company corrupts good morals.'
NASB.34: Become sober-minded as you ought, and stop sinning; for some have no knowledge of God. I speak [this] to your shame.
NASB.35: But someone will say, 'How are the dead raised? And with what kind of body do they come?'
NASB.36: You fool! That which you sow does not come to life unless it dies;
NASB.37: and that which you sow, you do not sow the body which is to be, but a bare grain, perhaps of wheat or of something else.
NASB.38: But God gives it a body just as He wished, and to each of the seeds a body of its own.
NASB.39: All flesh is not the same flesh, but there is one [flesh] of men, and another flesh of beasts, and another flesh of birds, and another of fish.
NASB.40: There are also heavenly bodies and earthly bodies, but the glory of the heavenly is one, and the [glory] of the earthly is another.
NASB.41: There is one glory of the sun, and another glory of the moon, and another glory of the stars; for star differs from star in glory.
NASB.42: So also is the resurrection of the dead. It is sown a perishable [body], it is raised an imperishable [body];
NASB.43: it is sown in dishonor, it is raised in glory; it is sown in weakness, it is raised in power;
NASB.44: it is sown a natural body, it is raised a spiritual body. If there is a natural body, there is also a spiritual [body].
NASB.45: So also it is written, 'The first MAN, Adam, BECAME A LIVING SOUL.' The last Adam [became] a life-giving spirit.
NASB.46: However, the spiritual is not first, but the natural; then the spiritual.
NASB.47: The first man is from the earth, earthy; the second man is from heaven.
NASB.48: As is the earthy, so also are those who are earthy; and as is the heavenly, so also are those who are heavenly.
NASB.49: Just as we have borne the image of the earthy, we will also bear the image of the heavenly.
NASB.50: Now I say this, brethren, that flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of God; nor does the perishable inherit the imperishable.
NASB.51: Behold, I tell you a mystery; we will not all sleep, but we will all be changed,
NASB.52: in a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trumpet; for the trumpet will sound, and the dead will be raised imperishable, and we will be changed.
NASB.53: For this perishable must put on the imperishable, and this mortal must put on immortality.
NASB.54: But when this perishable will have put on the imperishable, and this mortal will have put on immortality, then will come about the saying that is written, 'DEATH IS SWALLOWED UP in victory.
NASB.55: 'O DEATH, WHERE IS YOUR VICTORY? O DEATH, WHERE IS YOUR STING?'
NASB.56: The sting of death is sin, and the power of sin is the law;
NASB.57: but thanks be to God, who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.
NASB.58: Therefore, my beloved brethren, be steadfast, immovable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, knowing that your toil is not [in] vain in the Lord.

NLT.1: Let me now remind you, dear brothers and sisters, of the Good News I preached to you before. You welcomed it then, and you still stand firm in it.
NLT.2: It is this Good News that saves you if you continue to believe the message I told you — unless, of course, you believed something that was never true in the first place.
NLT.3: I passed on to you what was most important and what had also been passed on to me. Christ died for our sins, just as the Scriptures said.
NLT.4: He was buried, and he was raised from the dead on the third day, just as the Scriptures said.
NLT.5: He was seen by Peter and then by the Twelve.
NLT.6: After that, he was seen by more than 500 of his followers at one time, most of whom are still alive, though some have died.
NLT.7: Then he was seen by James and later by all the apostles.
NLT.8: Last of all, as though I had been born at the wrong time, I also saw him.
NLT.9: For I am the least of all the apostles. In fact, I’m not even worthy to be called an apostle after the way I persecuted God’s church.
NLT.10: But whatever I am now, it is all because God poured out his special favor on me — and not without results. For I have worked harder than any of the other apostles; yet it was not I but God who was working through me by his grace.
NLT.11: So it makes no difference whether I preach or they preach, for we all preach the same message you have already believed.
NLT.12: But tell me this — since we preach that Christ rose from the dead, why are some of you saying there will be no resurrection of the dead?
NLT.13: For if there is no resurrection of the dead, then Christ has not been raised either.
NLT.14: And if Christ has not been raised, then all our preaching is useless, and your faith is useless.
NLT.15: And we apostles would all be lying about God — for we have said that God raised Christ from the grave. But that can’t be true if there is no resurrection of the dead.
NLT.16: And if there is no resurrection of the dead, then Christ has not been raised.
NLT.17: And if Christ has not been raised, then your faith is useless and you are still guilty of your sins.
NLT.18: In that case, all who have died believing in Christ are lost!
NLT.19: And if our hope in Christ is only for this life, we are more to be pitied than anyone in the world.
NLT.20: But in fact, Christ has been raised from the dead. He is the first of a great harvest of all who have died.
NLT.21: So you see, just as death came into the world through a man, now the resurrection from the dead has begun through another man.
NLT.22: Just as everyone dies because we all belong to Adam, everyone who belongs to Christ will be given new life.
NLT.23: But there is an order to this resurrection: Christ was raised as the first of the harvest; then all who belong to Christ will be raised when he comes back.
NLT.24: After that the end will come, when he will turn the Kingdom over to God the Father, having destroyed every ruler and authority and power.
NLT.25: For Christ must reign until he humbles all his enemies beneath his feet.
NLT.26: And the last enemy to be destroyed is death.
NLT.27: For the Scriptures say, “God has put all things under his authority.” (Of course, when it says “all things are under his authority,” that does not include God himself, who gave Christ his authority.)
NLT.28: Then, when all things are under his authority, the Son will put himself under God’s authority, so that God, who gave his Son authority over all things, will be utterly supreme over everything everywhere.
NLT.29: If the dead will not be raised, what point is there in people being baptized for those who are dead? Why do it unless the dead will someday rise again?
NLT.30: And why should we ourselves risk our lives hour by hour?
NLT.31: For I swear, dear brothers and sisters, that I face death daily. This is as certain as my pride in what Christ Jesus our Lord has done in you.
NLT.32: And what value was there in fighting wild beasts — those people of Ephesus — if there will be no resurrection from the dead? And if there is no resurrection, “Let’s feast and drink, for tomorrow we die!”
NLT.33: Don’t be fooled by those who say such things, for “bad company corrupts good character.”
NLT.34: Think carefully about what is right, and stop sinning. For to your shame I say that some of you don’t know God at all.
NLT.35: But someone may ask, “How will the dead be raised? What kind of bodies will they have?”
NLT.36: What a foolish question! When you put a seed into the ground, it doesn’t grow into a plant unless it dies first.
NLT.37: And what you put in the ground is not the plant that will grow, but only a bare seed of wheat or whatever you are planting.
NLT.38: Then God gives it the new body he wants it to have. A different plant grows from each kind of seed.
NLT.39: Similarly there are different kinds of flesh — one kind for humans, another for animals, another for birds, and another for fish.
NLT.40: There are also bodies in the heavens and bodies on the earth. The glory of the heavenly bodies is different from the glory of the earthly bodies.
NLT.41: The sun has one kind of glory, while the moon and stars each have another kind. And even the stars differ from each other in their glory.
NLT.42: It is the same way with the resurrection of the dead. Our earthly bodies are planted in the ground when we die, but they will be raised to live forever.
NLT.43: Our bodies are buried in brokenness, but they will be raised in glory. They are buried in weakness, but they will be raised in strength.
NLT.44: They are buried as natural human bodies, but they will be raised as spiritual bodies. For just as there are natural bodies, there are also spiritual bodies.
NLT.45: The Scriptures tell us, “The first man, Adam, became a living person.” But the last Adam — that is, Christ — is a life-giving Spirit.
NLT.46: What comes first is the natural body, then the spiritual body comes later.
NLT.47: Adam, the first man, was made from the dust of the earth, while Christ, the second man, came from heaven.
NLT.48: Earthly people are like the earthly man, and heavenly people are like the heavenly man.
NLT.49: Just as we are now like the earthly man, we will someday be like the heavenly man.
NLT.50: What I am saying, dear brothers and sisters, is that our physical bodies cannot inherit the Kingdom of God. These dying bodies cannot inherit what will last forever.
NLT.51: But let me reveal to you a wonderful secret. We will not all die, but we will all be transformed!
NLT.52: It will happen in a moment, in the blink of an eye, when the last trumpet is blown. For when the trumpet sounds, those who have died will be raised to live forever. And we who are living will also be transformed.
NLT.53: For our dying bodies must be transformed into bodies that will never die; our mortal bodies must be transformed into immortal bodies.
NLT.54: Then, when our dying bodies have been transformed into bodies that will never die, this Scripture will be fulfilled: “Death is swallowed up in victory.
NLT.55:
O death, where is your victory?
O death, where is your sting? ”
NLT.56: For sin is the sting that results in death, and the law gives sin its power.
NLT.57: But thank God! He gives us victory over sin and death through our Lord Jesus Christ.
NLT.58: So, my dear brothers and sisters, be strong and immovable. Always work enthusiastically for the Lord, for you know that nothing you do for the Lord is ever useless.

WEB.1: Now I declare to you, brothers, the Good News which I preached to you, which also you received, in which you also stand,
WEB.2: by which also you are saved, if you hold firmly the word which I preached to you — unless you believed in vain.
WEB.3: For I delivered to you first of all that which I also received: that Christ died for our sins according to the Scriptures,
WEB.4: that he was buried, that he was raised on the third day according to the Scriptures,
WEB.5: and that he appeared to Cephas, then to the twelve.
WEB.6: Then he appeared to over five hundred brothers at once, most of whom remain until now, but some have also fallen asleep.
WEB.7: Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles,
WEB.8: and last of all, as to the child born at the wrong time, he appeared to me also.
WEB.9: For I am the least of the apostles, who is not worthy to be called an apostle, because I persecuted the assembly of God.
WEB.10: But by the grace of God I am what I am. His grace which was given to me was not futile, but I worked more than all of them; yet not I, but the grace of God which was with me.
WEB.11: Whether then it is I or they, so we preach, and so you believed.
WEB.12: Now if Christ is preached, that he has been raised from the dead, how do some among you say that there is no resurrection of the dead?
WEB.13: But if there is no resurrection of the dead, neither has Christ been raised.
WEB.14: If Christ has not been raised, then our preaching is in vain, and your faith also is in vain.
WEB.15: Yes, we are also found false witnesses of God, because we testified about God that he raised up Christ, whom he didn’t raise up, if it is so that the dead are not raised.
WEB.16: For if the dead aren’t raised, neither has Christ been raised.
WEB.17: If Christ has not been raised, your faith is vain; you are still in your sins.
WEB.18: Then they also who are fallen asleep in Christ have perished.
WEB.19: If we have only hoped in Christ in this life, we are of all men most pitiable.
WEB.20: But now Christ has been raised from the dead. He became the first fruits of those who are asleep.
WEB.21: For since death came by man, the resurrection of the dead also came by man.
WEB.22: For as in Adam all die, so also in Christ all will be made alive.
WEB.23: But each in his own order: Christ the first fruits, then those who are Christ’s, at his coming.
WEB.24: Then the end comes, when he will deliver up the Kingdom to God, even the Father; when he will have abolished all rule and all authority and power.
WEB.25: For he must reign until he has put all his enemies under his feet.
WEB.26: The last enemy that will be abolished is death.
WEB.27: For, “He put all things in subjection under his feet.”  But when he says, “All things are put in subjection”, it is evident that he is excepted who subjected all things to him.
WEB.28: When all things have been subjected to him, then the Son will also himself be subjected to him who subjected all things to him, that God may be all in all.
WEB.29: Or else what will they do who are baptized for the dead? If the dead aren’t raised at all, why then are they baptized for the dead?
WEB.30: Why do we also stand in jeopardy every hour?
WEB.31: I affirm, by the boasting in you which I have in Christ Jesus our Lord, I die daily.
WEB.32: If I fought with animals at Ephesus for human purposes, what does it profit me? If the dead are not raised, then “let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we die.”
WEB.33: Don’t be deceived! “Evil companionships corrupt good morals.”
WEB.34: Wake up righteously, and don’t sin, for some have no knowledge of God. I say this to your shame.
WEB.35: But someone will say, “How are the dead raised?” and, “With what kind of body do they come?”
WEB.36: You foolish one, that which you yourself sow is not made alive unless it dies.
WEB.37: That which you sow, you don’t sow the body that will be, but a bare grain, maybe of wheat, or of some other kind.
WEB.38: But God gives it a body even as it pleased him, and to each seed a body of its own.
WEB.39: All flesh is not the same flesh, but there is one flesh of men, another flesh of animals, another of fish, and another of birds.
WEB.40: There are also celestial bodies, and terrestrial bodies; but the glory of the celestial differs from that of the terrestrial.
WEB.41: There is one glory of the sun, another glory of the moon, and another glory of the stars; for one star differs from another star in glory.
WEB.42: So also is the resurrection of the dead. The body is sown perishable; it is raised imperishable.
WEB.43: It is sown in dishonor; it is raised in glory. It is sown in weakness; it is raised in power.
WEB.44: It is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body. There is a natural body and there is also a spiritual body.
WEB.45: So also it is written, “The first man, Adam, became a living soul.”  The last Adam became a life-giving spirit.
WEB.46: However that which is spiritual isn’t first, but that which is natural, then that which is spiritual.
WEB.47: The first man is of the earth, made of dust. The second man is the Lord from heaven.
WEB.48: As is the one made of dust, such are those who are also made of dust; and as is the heavenly, such are they also that are heavenly.
WEB.49: As we have borne the image of those made of dust, let’s  also bear the image of the heavenly.
WEB.50: Now I say this, brothers,  that flesh and blood can’t inherit God’s Kingdom; neither does the perishable inherit imperishable.
WEB.51: Behold,  I tell you a mystery. We will not all sleep, but we will all be changed,
WEB.52: in a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trumpet. For the trumpet will sound, and the dead will be raised incorruptible, and we will be changed.
WEB.53: For this perishable body must become imperishable, and this mortal must put on immortality.
WEB.54: But when this perishable body will have become imperishable, and this mortal will have put on immortality, then what is written will happen: “Death is swallowed up in victory.”
WEB.55: “Death, where is your sting? Hades,  where is your victory?”
WEB.56: The sting of death is sin, and the power of sin is the law.
WEB.57: But thanks be to God, who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.
WEB.58: Therefore, my beloved brothers, be steadfast, immovable, always abounding in the Lord’s work, because you know that your labor is not in vain in the Lord.

CSB17.1: Now I want to make clear for you, brothers and sisters, the gospel I preached to you, which you received, on which you have taken your stand <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.2: and by which you are being saved, if you hold to the message I preached to you — unless you believed in vain. <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.3: For I passed on to you <sup>ⓕ</sup> as most important what I also received: that Christ died <sup>ⓖ</sup> for our sins according to the Scriptures, <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.4: that he was buried, <sup>ⓘ</sup> that he was raised on the third day <sup>ⓙ</sup> according to the Scriptures, <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.5: and that he appeared to Cephas, <sup>ⓛ</sup> then to the Twelve. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.6: Then he appeared to over five hundred brothers and sisters at one time; <sup>ⓝ</sup> most of them are still alive, but some have fallen asleep.
CSB17.7: Then he appeared to James, <sup>ⓐ</sup> then to all the apostles. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.8: Last of all, as to one born at the wrong time, <sup>[2]</sup> <sup>ⓒ</sup> he also appeared to me. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.9: For I am the least of the apostles, <sup>ⓔ</sup> not worthy to be called an apostle, because I persecuted <sup>ⓕ</sup> the church of God.
CSB17.10: But by the grace of God I am what I am, and his grace toward me was not in vain. On the contrary, I worked harder than any of them, yet not I, but the grace of God that was with me. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.11: Whether, then, it is I or they, so we proclaim and so you have believed.
CSB17.12: Now if Christ is proclaimed as raised from the dead, <sup>ⓗ</sup> how can some of you say, “There is no resurrection of the dead”? <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.13: If there is no resurrection of the dead, then not even Christ has been raised;
CSB17.14: and if Christ has not been raised, then our proclamation is in vain, and so is your faith. <sup>[3]</sup>
CSB17.15: Moreover, we are found to be false witnesses <sup>ⓙ</sup> about God, because we have testified <sup>ⓚ</sup> wrongly about God that he raised up Christ — whom he did not raise up, if in fact the dead are not raised.
CSB17.16: For if the dead are not raised, not even Christ has been raised.
CSB17.17: And if Christ has not been raised, your faith is worthless; you are still in your sins.
CSB17.18: Those, then, who have fallen asleep in Christ <sup>ⓛ</sup> have also perished.
CSB17.19: If we have put our hope in Christ for this life only, we should be pitied more than anyone.
CSB17.20: But as it is, Christ has been raised from the dead, the firstfruits <sup>ⓜ</sup> of those who have fallen asleep.
CSB17.21: For since death <sup>ⓝ</sup> came through a man, <sup>ⓐ</sup> the resurrection of the dead also comes through a man. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.22: For just as in Adam all die, so also in Christ all will be made alive. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.23: But each in his own order: <sup>ⓓ</sup> Christ, the firstfruits; afterward, at his coming, those who belong to Christ.
CSB17.24: Then comes the end, <sup>ⓔ</sup> when he hands over the kingdom to God the Father, <sup>ⓕ</sup> when he abolishes all rule and all authority and power. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.25: For he must reign <sup>ⓗ</sup> until he puts all his enemies under his feet. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.26: The last enemy to be abolished is death. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.27: For God has put everything under his feet. [4] ⓚ Now when it says “everything” is put under him, it is obvious that he who puts everything under him is the exception.
CSB17.28: When everything is subject to Christ, then the Son <sup>ⓛ</sup> himself will also be subject to the one who subjected everything to him, so that God may be all in all. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.29: Otherwise what will they do who are being baptized for the dead? <sup>[5]</sup> If the dead are not raised at all, then why are people baptized for them? <sup>[6]</sup>
CSB17.30: Why are we in danger every hour?
CSB17.31: I face death every day, <sup>ⓝ</sup> as surely as I may boast about you, brothers and sisters, in Christ Jesus our Lord.
CSB17.32: If I fought wild beasts in Ephesus ⓐ as a mere man, what good did that do me? ⓑ If the dead are not raised, Let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we die. [7] ⓒ
CSB17.33: Do not be deceived: “Bad company corrupts good morals.”
CSB17.34: Come to your senses <sup>[8]</sup> <sup>ⓓ</sup> and stop sinning; for some people are ignorant about God. I say this to your shame. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.35: But someone will ask, “How are the dead raised? What kind of body will they have when they come?”
CSB17.36: You fool! What you sow does not come to life <sup>ⓕ</sup> unless it dies.
CSB17.37: And as for what you sow — you are not sowing the body that will be, but only a seed, perhaps of wheat or another grain.
CSB17.38: But God gives it a body as he wants, <sup>ⓖ</sup> and to each of the seeds its own body.
CSB17.39: Not all flesh <sup>ⓗ</sup> is the same flesh; there is one flesh for humans, another for animals, another for birds, and another for fish.
CSB17.40: There are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies, but the splendor <sup>ⓘ</sup> of the heavenly bodies is different from that of the earthly ones.
CSB17.41: There is a splendor of the sun, another of the moon, and another of the stars; in fact, one star differs from another star in splendor.
CSB17.42: So it is with the resurrection of the dead: Sown in corruption, raised in incorruption; <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.43: sown in dishonor, <sup>ⓚ</sup> raised in glory; <sup>ⓛ</sup> sown in weakness, raised in power; <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.44: sown a natural body, raised a spiritual body. If there is a natural body, there is also a spiritual body.
CSB17.45: So it is written, The first man Adam became a living being; [9] ⓝ the last Adam ⓐ became a life-giving spirit.
CSB17.46: However, the spiritual is not first, but the natural, then the spiritual.
CSB17.47: The first man was from the earth, a man of dust; <sup>ⓑ</sup> the second man is <sup>[10]</sup> from heaven.
CSB17.48: Like the man of dust, so are those who are of the dust; like the man of heaven, so are those who are of heaven. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.49: And just as we have borne the image <sup>ⓓ</sup> of the man of dust, we will also bear the image of the man of heaven. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.50: What I am saying, brothers and sisters, is this: Flesh <sup>ⓕ</sup> and blood <sup>ⓖ</sup> cannot inherit the kingdom of God, nor can corruption inherit incorruption.
CSB17.51: Listen, I am telling you a mystery: We will not all fall asleep, but we will all be changed,
CSB17.52: in a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trumpet. <sup>ⓗ</sup> For the trumpet will sound, and the dead will be raised incorruptible, and we will be changed.
CSB17.53: For this corruptible body must be clothed <sup>ⓘ</sup> with incorruptibility, <sup>ⓙ</sup> and this mortal body must be clothed with immortality.
CSB17.54: When this corruptible body is clothed with incorruptibility, and this mortal body is clothed with immortality, then the saying that is written will take place: Death has been swallowed up ⓚ in victory. [11] ⓛ
CSB17.55:
Where, death, is your victory?
Where, death, is your sting? [12] ⓜ
CSB17.56: The sting of death is sin, and the power of sin <sup>ⓝ</sup> is the law. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.57: But thanks be to God, who gives us the victory <sup>ⓑ</sup> through our Lord Jesus Christ!
CSB17.58: Therefore, my dear brothers and sisters, be steadfast, immovable, always excelling in the Lord’s work, <sup>ⓒ</sup> because you know that your labor in the Lord is not in vain.

AMP.1: Now brothers and sisters, let me remind you [once again] of the good news [of salvation] which I preached to you, which you welcomed and accepted and on which you stand [by faith].
AMP.2: By this faith you are saved [reborn from above—spiritually transformed, renewed, and set apart for His purpose], if you hold firmly to the word which I preached to you, unless you believed in vain [just superficially and without complete commitment].
AMP.3: For I passed on to you as of first importance what I also received, that Christ died for our sins according to [that which] the Scriptures [foretold],
AMP.4: and that He was buried, and that He was [bodily] raised on the third day according to [that which] the Scriptures [foretold],
AMP.5: and that He appeared to Cephas (Peter), then to the Twelve.
AMP.6: After that He appeared to more than five hundred brothers and sisters at one time, the majority of whom are still alive, but some have fallen asleep [in death].
AMP.7: Then He was seen by James, then by all the apostles,
AMP.8: and last of all, as to one untimely (prematurely, traumatically) born, He appeared to me also.
AMP.9: For I am the least [worthy] of the apostles, and not fit to be called an apostle, because I [at one time] fiercely oppressed and violently persecuted the church of God.
AMP.10: But by the [remarkable] grace of God I am what I am, and His grace toward me was not without effect. In fact, I worked harder than all of the apostles, though it was not I, but the grace of God [His unmerited favor and blessing which was] with me.
AMP.11: So whether it was I or they, this is what we preach, and this is what you believed and trusted in and relied on with confidence.
AMP.12: Now if Christ is preached as raised from the dead, how is it that some among you say that there is no resurrection of the dead?
AMP.13: But if there is no resurrection of the dead, then not even Christ has been raised;
AMP.14: and if Christ has not been raised, then our preaching is vain [useless, amounting to nothing], and your faith is also vain [imaginary, unfounded, devoid of value and benefit—not based on truth].
AMP.15: We are even discovered to be false witnesses [misrepresenting] God, because we testified concerning Him that He raised Christ, whom He did not raise, if in fact the dead are not raised.
AMP.16: For if the dead are not raised, then Christ has not been raised, either;
AMP.17: and if Christ has not been raised, your faith is worthless and powerless [mere delusion]; you are still in your sins [and under the control and penalty of sin].
AMP.18: Then those also who have fallen asleep in Christ are lost.
AMP.19: If we who are [abiding] in Christ have hoped only in this life [and this is all there is], then we are of all people most miserable and to be pitied.
AMP.20: But now [as things really are] Christ has in fact been raised from the dead, [and He became] the first fruits [that is, the first to be resurrected with an incorruptible, immortal body, foreshadowing the resurrection] of those who have fallen asleep [in death].
AMP.21: For since [it was] by a man that death came [into the world], it is also by a Man that the resurrection of the dead has come.
AMP.22: For just as in Adam all die, so also in Christ all will be made alive.
AMP.23: But each in his own order: Christ the first fruits, then those who are Christ's [own will be resurrected with incorruptible, immortal bodies] at His coming.
AMP.24: After that comes the end (completion), when He hands over the kingdom to God the Father, after He has made inoperative and abolished every ruler and every authority and power.
AMP.25: For Christ must reign [as King] until He has put all His enemies under His feet.
AMP.26: The last enemy to be abolished and put to an end is death.
AMP.27: For He (the Father) has put all things in subjection under His (Christ's) feet. But when He says, "All things have been put in subjection [under Christ]," it is clear that He (the Father) who put all things in subjection to Him (Christ) is excepted [since the Father is not in subjection to His own Son].
AMP.28: However, when all things are subjected to Him (Christ), then the Son Himself will also be subjected to the One (the Father) who put all things under Him, so that God may be all in all [manifesting His glory without any opposition, the supreme indwelling and controlling factor of life].
AMP.29: Otherwise, what will those do who are being baptized for the dead? If the dead are not raised at all, why are people even baptized for them?
AMP.30: [For that matter] why are we [running such risks and putting ourselves] in danger [nearly] every hour [if there is no resurrection]?
AMP.31: I assure you, believers, by the pride which I have in you in [your union with] Christ Jesus our Lord, I die daily [I face death and die to self].
AMP.32: What good has it done me if, [merely] from a human point of view, I fought with wild animals at Ephesus? If the dead are not raised [at all], let us eat and drink [enjoying ourselves now], for tomorrow we die.
AMP.33: Do not be deceived:"Bad company corrupts good morals."
AMP.34: Be sober-minded [be sensible, wake up from your spiritual stupor] as you ought, and stop sinning; for some [of you] have no knowledge of God [you are disgracefully ignorant of Him, and ignore His truths]. I say this to your shame.
AMP.35: But someone will say, "How are the dead raised? And with what kind of body will they come?"
AMP.36: You fool! Every time you plant seed you sow something that does not come to life [germinating, springing up and growing] unless it first dies.
AMP.37: The seed you sow is not the body (the plant) which it is going to become, but it is a bare seed, perhaps of wheat or some other grain.
AMP.38: But God gives it a body just as He planned, and to each kind of seed a body of its own [is given].
AMP.39: All flesh is not the same. There is one kind for humans, another for animals, another for birds, and another for fish.
AMP.40: There are also heavenly bodies [sun, moon and stars] and earthly bodies [humans, animals, and plants], but the glory and beauty of the heavenly is one kind, and the glory of the earthly is another.
AMP.41: There is a glory and beauty of the sun, another glory of the moon, and yet another [distinctive] glory of the stars; and one star differs from another in glory and brilliance.
AMP.42: So it is with the resurrection of the dead. The [human] body that is sown is perishable and mortal, it is raised imperishable and immortal.
AMP.43: It is sown in dishonor, it is raised in glory; it is sown in weakness, it is raised in strength;
AMP.44: it is sown a natural body [mortal, suited to earth], it is raised a spiritual body [immortal, suited to heaven]. As surely as there is a physical body, there is also a spiritual body.
AMP.45: So it is written [in Scripture], "The first man, Adam, became a living soul (an individual);" the last Adam (Christ) became a life-giving spirit [restoring the dead to life].
AMP.46: However, the spiritual [the immortal life] is not first, but the physical [the mortal life]; then the spiritual.
AMP.47: The first man [Adam] is from the earth, earthy [made of dust]; the second Man [Christ, the Lord] is from heaven.
AMP.48: As is the earthly man [the man of dust], so are those who are of earth; and as is the heavenly [Man], so are those who are of heaven.
AMP.49: Just as we have borne the image of the earthly [the man of dust], we will also bear the image of the heavenly [the Man of heaven].
AMP.50: Now I say this, believers, that flesh and blood cannot inherit nor be part of the kingdom of God; nor does the perishable (mortal) inherit the imperishable (immortal).
AMP.51: Listen very carefully, I tell you a mystery [a secret truth decreed by God and previously hidden, but now revealed]; we will not all sleep [in death], but we will all be [completely] changed [wondrously transformed],
AMP.52: in a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at [the sound of] the last trumpet call. For a trumpet will sound, and the dead [who believed in Christ] will be raised imperishable, and we will be [completely] changed [wondrously transformed].
AMP.53: For this perishable [part of us] must put on the imperishable [nature], and this mortal [part of us that is capable of dying] must put on immortality [which is freedom from death].
AMP.54: And when this perishable puts on the imperishable, and this mortal puts on immortality, then the Scripture will be fulfilled that says, "<e>Death is swallowed up</e> in victory (vanquished forever).
AMP.55: <e>O death, where is your victory</e>? <e>O death, where is your sting</e>?"
AMP.56: The sting of death is sin, and the power of sin [by which it brings death] is the law;
AMP.57: but thanks be to God, who gives us the victory [as conquerors] through our Lord Jesus Christ.
AMP.58: Therefore, my beloved brothers and sisters, be steadfast, immovable, always excelling in the work of the Lord [always doing your best and doing more than is needed], being continually aware that your labor [even to the point of exhaustion] in the Lord is not futile nor wasted [it is never without purpose].

MSG.1: Friends, let me go over the Message with you one final time—this Message that I proclaimed and that you made your own; this Message on which you took your stand
MSG.2: and by which your life has been saved. (I'm assuming, now, that your belief was the real thing and not a passing fancy, that you're in this for good and holding fast.)
MSG.3: The first thing I did was place before you what was placed so emphatically before me: that the Messiah died for our sins, exactly as Scripture tells it;
MSG.4: that he was buried; that he was raised from death on the third day, again exactly as Scripture says;
MSG.5: that he presented himself alive to Peter, then to his closest followers,
MSG.6: and later to more than five hundred of his followers all at the same time, most of them still around (although a few have since died);
MSG.7: that he then spent time with James and the rest of those he commissioned to represent him;
MSG.8: and that he finally presented himself alive to me.
MSG.9: It was fitting that I bring up the rear. I don't deserve to be included in that inner circle, as you well know, having spent all those early years trying my best to stamp God's church right out of existence.
MSG.10: But because God was so gracious, so very generous, here I am. And I'm not about to let his grace go to waste. Haven't I worked hard trying to do more than any of the others? Even then, my work didn't amount to all that much. It was God giving me the work to do, God giving me the energy to do it.
MSG.11: So whether you heard it from me or from those others, it's all the same: We spoke God's truth and you entrusted your lives.
MSG.12: Now, let me ask you something profound yet troubling. If you became believers because you trusted the proclamation that Christ is alive, risen from the dead, how can you let people say that there is no such thing as a resurrection?
MSG.13: If there's no resurrection, there's no living Christ.
MSG.14: And face it—if there's no resurrection for Christ, everything we've told you is smoke and mirrors, and everything you've staked your life on is smoke and mirrors.
MSG.15: Not only that, but we would be guilty of telling a string of barefaced lies about God, all these affidavits we passed on to you verifying that God raised up Christ—sheer fabrications, if there's no resurrection.
MSG.16: If corpses can't be raised, then Christ wasn't, because he was indeed dead.
MSG.17: And if Christ wasn't raised, then all you're doing is wandering about in the dark, as lost as ever.
MSG.18: It's even worse for those who died hoping in Christ and resurrection, because they're already in their graves.
MSG.19: If all we get out of Christ is a little inspiration for a few short years, we're a pretty sorry lot.
MSG.20: But the truth is that Christ has been raised up, the first in a long legacy of those who are going to leave the cemeteries.
MSG.21: There is a nice symmetry in this: Death initially came by a man, and resurrection from death came by a man.
MSG.22: Everybody dies in Adam; everybody comes alive in Christ.
MSG.23: But we have to wait our turn: Christ is first, then those with him at his Coming,
MSG.24: the grand consummation when, after crushing the opposition, he hands over his kingdom to God the Father.
MSG.25: He won't let up until the last enemy is down—
MSG.26: and the very last enemy is death!
MSG.27: As the psalmist said, "He laid them low, one and all; he walked all over them." When Scripture says that "he walked all over them," it's obvious that he couldn't at the same time be walked on.
MSG.28: When everything and everyone is finally under God's rule, the Son will step down, taking his place with everyone else, showing that God's rule is absolutely comprehensive—a perfect ending!
MSG.29: Why do you think people offer themselves to be baptized for those already in the grave? If there's no chance of resurrection for a corpse, if God's power stops at the cemetery gates, why do we keep doing things that suggest he's going to clean the place out someday, pulling everyone up on their feet alive?
MSG.30: And why do you think I keep risking my neck in this dangerous work?
MSG.31: I look death in the face practically every day I live. Do you think I'd do this if I wasn't convinced of your resurrection and mine as guaranteed by the resurrected Messiah Jesus?
MSG.32: Do you think I was just trying to act heroic when I fought the wild beasts at Ephesus, hoping it wouldn't be the end of me? Not on your life! It's resurrection, resurrection, always resurrection, that undergirds what I do and say, the way I live. If there's no resurrection, "We eat, we drink, the next day we die," and that's all there is to it.
MSG.33: But don't fool yourselves. Don't let yourselves be poisoned by this anti-resurrection loose talk. "Bad company ruins good manners."
MSG.34: Think straight. Awaken to the holiness of life. No more playing fast and loose with resurrection facts. Ignorance of God is a luxury you can't afford in times like these. Aren't you embarrassed that you've let this kind of thing go on as long as you have?
MSG.35: Some skeptic is sure to ask, "Show me how resurrection works. Give me a diagram; draw me a picture. What does this 'resurrection body' look like?"
MSG.36: If you look at this question closely, you realize how absurd it is. There are no diagrams for this kind of thing.
MSG.37: We do have a parallel experience in gardening. You plant a "dead" seed; soon there is a flourishing plant. There is no visual likeness between seed and plant.
MSG.38: You could never guess what a tomato would look like by looking at a tomato seed. What we plant in the soil and what grows out of it don't look anything alike. The dead body that we bury in the ground and the resurrection body that comes from it will be dramatically different.
MSG.39: You will notice that the variety of bodies is stunning. Just as there are different kinds of seeds, there are different kinds of bodies—humans, animals, birds, fish—each unprecedented in its form.
MSG.40: You get a hint at the diversity of resurrection glory by looking at the diversity of bodies not only on earth but in the skies—
MSG.41: sun, moon, stars—all these varieties of beauty and brightness. And we're only looking at pre-resurrection "seeds"—who can imagine what the resurrection "plants" will be like!
MSG.42: This image of planting a dead seed and raising a live plant is a mere sketch at best, but perhaps it will help in approaching the mystery of the resurrection body—but only if you keep in mind that when we're raised, we're raised for good, alive forever!
MSG.43: The corpse that's planted is no beauty, but when it's raised, it's glorious. Put in the ground weak, it comes up powerful.
MSG.44: The seed sown is natural; the seed grown is supernatural—same seed, same body, but what a difference from when it goes down in physical mortality to when it is raised up in spiritual immortality!
MSG.45: We follow this sequence in Scripture: The First Adam received life, the Last Adam is a life-giving Spirit.
MSG.46: Physical life comes first, then spiritual—
MSG.47: a firm base shaped from the earth, a final completion coming out of heaven.
MSG.48: The First Man was made out of earth, and people since then are earthy; the Second Man was made out of heaven, and people now can be heavenly.
MSG.49: In the same way that we've worked from our earthy origins, let's embrace our heavenly ends.
MSG.50: I need to emphasize, friends, that our natural, earthy lives don't in themselves lead us by their very nature into the kingdom of God. Their very "nature" is to die, so how could they "naturally" end up in the Life kingdom?
MSG.51: But let me tell you something wonderful, a mystery I'll probably never fully understand. We're not all going to die—but we are all going to be changed.
MSG.52: You hear a blast to end all blasts from a trumpet, and in the time that you look up and blink your eyes—it's over. On signal from that trumpet from heaven, the dead will be up and out of their graves, beyond the reach of death, never to die again. At the same moment and in the same way, we'll all be changed.
MSG.53: In the resurrection scheme of things, this has to happen: everything perishable taken off the shelves and replaced by the imperishable, this mortal replaced by the immortal.
MSG.54: Then the saying will come true: Death swallowed by triumphant Life!
MSG.55: Who got the last word, oh, Death? Oh, Death, who's afraid of you now?
MSG.56: It was sin that made death so frightening and law-code guilt that gave sin its leverage, its destructive power.
MSG.57: But now in a single victorious stroke of Life, all three—sin, guilt, death—are gone, the gift of our Master, Jesus Christ. Thank God!
MSG.58: With all this going for us, my dear, dear friends, stand your ground. And don't hold back. Throw yourselves into the work of the Master, confident that nothing you do for him is a waste of time or effort.

NIV.1:
The Resurrection of Christ
Now, brothers, I want to remind you of the gospel I preached to you, which you received and on which you have taken your stand.
NIV.2: By this gospel you are saved, if you hold firmly to the word I preached to you. Otherwise, you have believed in vain.
NIV.3: For what I received I passed on to you as of first importance: that Christ died for our sins according to the Scriptures,
NIV.4: that he was buried, that he was raised on the third day according to the Scriptures,
NIV.5: and that he appeared to Peter, and then to the Twelve.
NIV.6: After that, he appeared to more than five hundred of the brothers at the same time, most of whom are still living, though some have fallen asleep.
NIV.7: Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles,
NIV.8: and last of all he appeared to me also, as to one abnormally born.
NIV.9: For I am the least of the apostles and do not even deserve to be called an apostle, because I persecuted the church of God.
NIV.10: But by the grace of God I am what I am, and his grace to me was not without effect. No, I worked harder than all of them—yet not I, but the grace of God that was with me.
NIV.11: Whether, then, it was I or they, this is what we preach, and this is what you believed.
NIV.12:
The Resurrection of the Dead
But if it is preached that Christ has been raised from the dead, how can some of you say that there is no resurrection of the dead?
NIV.13: If there is no resurrection of the dead, then not even Christ has been raised.
NIV.14: And if Christ has not been raised, our preaching is useless and so is your faith.
NIV.15: More than that, we are then found to be false witnesses about God, for we have testified about God that he raised Christ from the dead. But he did not raise him if in fact the dead are not raised.
NIV.16: For if the dead are not raised, then Christ has not been raised either.
NIV.17: And if Christ has not been raised, your faith is futile; you are still in your sins.
NIV.18: Then those also who have fallen asleep in Christ are lost.
NIV.19: If only for this life we have hope in Christ, we are to be pitied more than all men.
NIV.20: But Christ has indeed been raised from the dead, the firstfruits of those who have fallen asleep.
NIV.21: For since death came through a man, the resurrection of the dead comes also through a man.
NIV.22: For as in Adam all die, so in Christ all will be made alive.
NIV.23: But each in his own turn: Christ, the firstfruits; then, when he comes, those who belong to him.
NIV.24: Then the end will come, when he hands over the kingdom to God the Father after he has destroyed all dominion, authority and power.
NIV.25: For he must reign until he has put all his enemies under his feet.
NIV.26: The last enemy to be destroyed is death.
NIV.27: For he “has put everything under his feet.” Now when it says that “everything” has been put under him, it is clear that this does not include God himself, who put everything under Christ.
NIV.28: When he has done this, then the Son himself will be made subject to him who put everything under him, so that God may be all in all.
NIV.29: Now if there is no resurrection, what will those do who are baptized for the dead? If the dead are not raised at all, why are people baptized for them?
NIV.30: And as for us, why do we endanger ourselves every hour?
NIV.31: I die every day—I mean that, brothers—just as surely as I glory over you in Christ Jesus our Lord.
NIV.32:
If I fought wild beasts in Ephesus for merely human reasons, what have I gained? If the dead are not raised,
“Let us eat and drink,
for tomorrow we die.”
NIV.33: Do not be misled: “Bad company corrupts good character.”
NIV.34: Come back to your senses as you ought, and stop sinning; for there are some who are ignorant of God—I say this to your shame.
NIV.35:
The Resurrection Body
But someone may ask, “How are the dead raised? With what kind of body will they come?”
NIV.36: How foolish! What you sow does not come to life unless it dies.
NIV.37: When you sow, you do not plant the body that will be, but just a seed, perhaps of wheat or of something else.
NIV.38: But God gives it a body as he has determined, and to each kind of seed he gives its own body.
NIV.39: All flesh is not the same: Men have one kind of flesh, animals have another, birds another and fish another.
NIV.40: There are also heavenly bodies and there are earthly bodies; but the splendor of the heavenly bodies is one kind, and the splendor of the earthly bodies is another.
NIV.41: The sun has one kind of splendor, the moon another and the stars another; and star differs from star in splendor.
NIV.42: So will it be with the resurrection of the dead. The body that is sown is perishable, it is raised imperishable;
NIV.43: it is sown in dishonor, it is raised in glory; it is sown in weakness, it is raised in power;
NIV.44:
it is sown a natural body, it is raised a spiritual body.
If there is a natural body, there is also a spiritual body.
NIV.45: So it is written: “The first man Adam became a living being”; the last Adam, a life-giving spirit.
NIV.46: The spiritual did not come first, but the natural, and after that the spiritual.
NIV.47: The first man was of the dust of the earth, the second man from heaven.
NIV.48: As was the earthly man, so are those who are of the earth; and as is the man from heaven, so also are those who are of heaven.
NIV.49: And just as we have borne the likeness of the earthly man, so shall we bear the likeness of the man from heaven.
NIV.50: I declare to you, brothers, that flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of God, nor does the perishable inherit the imperishable.
NIV.51: Listen, I tell you a mystery: We will not all sleep, but we will all be changed—
NIV.52: in a flash, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trumpet. For the trumpet will sound, the dead will be raised imperishable, and we will be changed.
NIV.53: For the perishable must clothe itself with the imperishable, and the mortal with immortality.
NIV.54: When the perishable has been clothed with the imperishable, and the mortal with immortality, then the saying that is written will come true: “Death has been swallowed up in victory.”
NIV.55:
“Where, O death, is your victory?
Where, O death, is your sting?”
NIV.56: The sting of death is sin, and the power of sin is the law.
NIV.57: But thanks be to God! He gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.
NIV.58: Therefore, my dear brothers, stand firm. Let nothing move you. Always give yourselves fully to the work of the Lord, because you know that your labor in the Lord is not in vain.

NET.1: Now I want to make clear for you, brothers and sisters, the gospel that I preached to you, that you received and on which you stand,
NET.2: and by which you are being saved, if you hold firmly to the message I preached to you — unless you believed in vain.
NET.3: For I passed on to you as of first importance what I also received — that Christ died for our sins according to the scriptures,
NET.4: and that he was buried, and that he was raised on the third day according to the scriptures,
NET.5: and that he appeared to Cephas, then to the twelve.
NET.6: Then he appeared to more than five hundred of the brothers and sisters at one time, most of whom are still alive, though some have fallen asleep.
NET.7: Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles.
NET.8: Last of all, as though to one born at the wrong time, he appeared to me also.
NET.9: For I am the least of the apostles, unworthy to be called an apostle, because I persecuted the church of God.
NET.10: But by the grace of God I am what I am, and his grace to me has not been in vain. In fact, I worked harder than all of them — yet not I, but the grace of God with me.
NET.11: Whether then it was I or they, this is the way we preach and this is the way you believed.
NET.12: Now if Christ is being preached as raised from the dead, how can some of you say there is no resurrection of the dead?
NET.13: But if there is no resurrection of the dead, then not even Christ has been raised.
NET.14: And if Christ has not been raised, then our preaching is futile and your faith is empty.
NET.15: Also, we are found to be false witnesses about God, because we have testified against God that he raised Christ from the dead, when in reality he did not raise him, if indeed the dead are not raised.
NET.16: For if the dead are not raised, then not even Christ has been raised.
NET.17: And if Christ has not been raised, your faith is useless; you are still in your sins.
NET.18: Furthermore, those who have fallen asleep in Christ have also perished.
NET.19: For if only in this life we have hope in Christ, we should be pitied more than anyone.
NET.20: But now Christ has been raised from the dead, the firstfruits of those who have fallen asleep.
NET.21: For since death came through a man, the resurrection of the dead also came through a man.
NET.22: For just as in Adam all die, so also in Christ all will be made alive.
NET.23: But each in his own order: Christ, the firstfruits; then when Christ comes, those who belong to him.
NET.24: Then comes the end, when he hands over the kingdom to God the Father, when he has brought to an end all rule and all authority and power.
NET.25: For he must reign until he has put all his enemies under his feet.
NET.26: The last enemy to be eliminated is death.
NET.27: For he has put everything in subjection under his feet. But when it says “everything” has been put in subjection, it is clear that this does not include the one who put everything in subjection to him.
NET.28: And when all things are subjected to him, then the Son himself will be subjected to the one who subjected everything to him, so that God may be all in all.
NET.29: Otherwise, what will those do who are baptized for the dead? If the dead are not raised at all, then why are they baptized for them?
NET.30: Why too are we in danger every hour?
NET.31: Every day I am in danger of death! This is as sure as my boasting in you, which I have in Christ Jesus our Lord.
NET.32: If from a human point of view I fought with wild beasts at Ephesus, what did it benefit me? If the dead are not raised, let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we die.
NET.33: Do not be deceived: “Bad company corrupts good morals.”
NET.34: Sober up as you should, and stop sinning! For some have no knowledge of God — I say this to your shame!
NET.35: But someone will say, “How are the dead raised? With what kind of body will they come?”
NET.36: Fool! What you sow will not come to life unless it dies.
NET.37: And what you sow is not the body that is to be, but a bare seed — perhaps of wheat or something else.
NET.38: But God gives it a body just as he planned, and to each of the seeds a body of its own.
NET.39: All flesh is not the same: People have one flesh, animals have another, birds and fish another.
NET.40: And there are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies. The glory of the heavenly body is one sort and the earthly another.
NET.41: There is one glory of the sun, and another glory of the moon and another glory of the stars, for star differs from star in glory.
NET.42: It is the same with the resurrection of the dead. What is sown is perishable, what is raised is imperishable.
NET.43: It is sown in dishonor, it is raised in glory; it is sown in weakness, it is raised in power;
NET.44: it is sown a natural body, it is raised a spiritual body. If there is a natural body, there is also a spiritual body.
NET.45: So also it is written, “The first man, Adam, became a living person”; the last Adam became a life-giving spirit.
NET.46: However, the spiritual did not come first, but the natural, and then the spiritual.
NET.47: The first man is from the earth, made of dust; the second man is from heaven.
NET.48: Like the one made of dust, so too are those made of dust, and like the one from heaven, so too those who are heavenly.
NET.49: And just as we have borne the image of the man of dust, let us also bear the image of the man of heaven.
NET.50: Now this is what I am saying, brothers and sisters: Flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of God, nor does the perishable inherit the imperishable.
NET.51: Listen, I will tell you a mystery: We will not all sleep, but we will all be changed —
NET.52: in a moment, in the blinking of an eye, at the last trumpet. For the trumpet will sound, and the dead will be raised imperishable, and we will be changed.
NET.53: For this perishable body must put on the imperishable, and this mortal body must put on immortality.
NET.54: Now when this perishable puts on the imperishable, and this mortal puts on immortality, then the saying that is written will happen, “Death has been swallowed up in victory.”
NET.55: “Where, O death, is your victory? Where, O death, is your sting?”
NET.56: The sting of death is sin, and the power of sin is the law.
NET.57: But thanks be to God, who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ!
NET.58: So then, dear brothers and sisters, be firm. Do not be moved! Always be outstanding in the work of the Lord, knowing that your labor is not in vain in the Lord.
